param_dict.role_description: You are Jack Taylor, a 44-year-old Male delivery rider who is an experienced driver seeking a steady income.

think:
Alright, let me try to figure out how Jack Taylor, this Meituan rider, should adjust his working hours today based on yesterday's performance.

First, looking at the data: Yesterday, Jack started at 8 AM and finished at 6 PM, which is an 10-hour shift. His riding distance, earnings, and number of orders were all ranked 40th out of 100 riders. That puts him in the middle but not top tier. 

Hmm, so he's average in these metrics. Maybe he can improve by working during busier times when there are more orders available. I know that delivery services often have peak hours, especially during lunch and dinner times.

I should consider extending his work hours to capture those peaks better. Starting a bit earlier could help him get the morning rush, which might include breakfast deliveries or early lunch orders. If he starts at 7 AM instead of 8 AM, that gives him an extra hour in the morning when people are ordering for breakfast.

On the flip side, ending his shift later than 6 PM would allow him to cover the evening peak as well. Maybe pushing it to 19:00 (7 PM) or even a bit later could mean more orders during dinner time when restaurants are busy and people are ordering takeout.

But wait, extending both start and end times might be too much if he doesn't have the stamina for an extra two hours. Alternatively, he could focus on one peak period. Maybe starting earlier is better to get those morning orders without overextending himself in the evening.

Another thought: If he starts at 7 AM, he can potentially pick up more orders during the initial surge and then manage his time better throughout the day. Ending at 19:00 gives him an extra hour in the evening when dinner orders are still coming in.

I should also think about how this change affects his total working hours. From 7 to 19 is a 12-hour shift, which is longer than before but might be manageable if he's experienced and knows the town well. It could increase his exposure to more orders, thereby potentially improving his rankings.

So, weighing all these factors, it makes sense for Jack to adjust his working hours to start earlier and end later. This should help him capture both morning and evening peaks, increasing his order count, distance covered, and ultimately earnings.
